templates.name: Flat-base weld fixture
intended_product_family: fabricated assemblies with a defensible primary support orientation
processes: cobot or robot welding when separately validated

production_volume_range.basis: Project quantity drives simplicity, durability, and automation tradeoffs.

expected_datum_opportunities: edges or formed features away from weld distortion
support_strategy: three or more justified discrete supports with explicit load and distortion intent
locator_strategy: independent secondary and tertiary contacts tied to immutable product references
stop_strategy: replaceable stop that preserves loading and post-weld release
clamp_strategy: clamp toward named supports and keep joint corridors open
base_structure_strategy: plate or connected rail frame selected from size, access, shop capability, and volume
loading_unloading_model: load, seat, locate, clamp, process, release, and unload are distinct checked states
access_expectations: required joints, tacks, operator actions, tools, cleaning, and finished-product release remain clear
manufacturable_construction: standard tooling, laser-cut plates, formed brackets, tubes, and limited machining
process_context_needs: torch or hand envelope when access is not obvious
table or positioner when mounting or motion matters

configuration_parameters.name: build_orientation
configuration_parameters.description: Accepted manufacturing frame linked to source identity.

configuration_parameters.name: production_quantity
configuration_parameters.description: Quantity basis for cost and durability decisions.

required_human_review_questions: Is the locating scheme stable without fighting variation?
Can every required weld and unload action be completed?
Is the structure practical to build and maintain?

templates.name: Tack and location fixture
intended_product_family: assemblies that transfer to another process after initial location and tack

production_volume_range.basis: Transfer frequency and repeatability determine construction effort.

expected_datum_opportunities: assembly interfaces needed to establish pre-tack relationship
support_strategy: support components through fit-up without hiding required tack regions
locator_strategy: locate constituent parts independently where needed
stop_strategy: use stops that permit removal of the tacked assembly
clamp_strategy: use minimum hold-down needed for fit-up without creating transfer traps
base_structure_strategy: simple plate, rail, or modular base suited to transfer handling
loading_unloading_model: load parts, locate, clamp, tack, cool as required, release, transfer
access_expectations: all specified tack locations and release directions remain accessible
manufacturable_construction: prefer simple fabricated and reusable elements

configuration_parameters.name: tack_sequence
configuration_parameters.description: Project-approved tack intent and access sequence.

known_failure_modes: fixture supports only the final shape but not individual parts
tack access is assumed from full-weld access
required_human_review_questions: Will the tacked assembly retain required relationship during transfer?
Are tack locations and release sequence practical?

templates.name: Multi-station weld fixture
intended_product_family: small repeatable fabricated assemblies suited to repeated product-specific nests

production_volume_range.basis: Station count follows takt, access, envelope, and handling evidence.

expected_datum_opportunities: one validated nest definition repeated with station-specific source references
support_strategy: repeat role-consistent supports with station identities and reaction mappings
locator_strategy: repeat the approved datum logic without erasing station-specific evidence
stop_strategy: preserve individual loading and unloading at every station
clamp_strategy: provide open and closed states and avoid cross-station access interference
base_structure_strategy: continuous mounted base or connected frame with explicit station parent paths
loading_unloading_model: define one-up and aggregate station flow, including partial occupancy when permitted
access_expectations: adjacent products, clamps, structure, and process equipment are included in access checks
manufacturable_construction: repeatable modules tied to a connected fabricated base
applicable_purchased_tooling_categories: pneumatic or hydraulic devices when approved
process_context_needs: tool or automation envelope when applicable

configuration_parameters.name: station_count
configuration_parameters.description: Requested count and deterministic feasible alternatives.

configuration_parameters.name: station_pitch
configuration_parameters.description: Derived or engineer-set pitch with clearance evidence.

required_human_review_questions: Is the station rhythm practical?
Does the connected base remain manufacturable and serviceable?
Are all stations reachable without unsafe or awkward handling?

templates.name: Assembly fixture
intended_product_family: mechanical assemblies requiring controlled relationship during fastening, bonding, or insertion
processes: press or slip insertion when separately validated

production_volume_range.basis: Volume and changeover needs determine modularity.

support_strategy: support each component through the assembly sequence
locator_strategy: locate components without blocking mating motion
stop_strategy: use insertion or seating stops only with defined reaction paths
clamp_strategy: hold assembled state without masking incomplete seating
base_structure_strategy: open frame, plate, or nest arranged around operator and tool access
loading_unloading_model: sequence constituent parts, tools, fasteners, release, and completed assembly removal
access_expectations: fastener, adhesive, insertion, inspection, and hand access are explicit
manufacturable_construction: simple reusable modules and replaceable product contacts
process_context_needs: operator and assembly-tool envelopes when access is constrained
unsupported_conditions: unmodeled press force or adhesive process claims
required_human_review_questions: Can each component and tool enter in sequence?
How is complete seating verified?

templates.name: Inspection fixture
intended_product_family: parts and assemblies needing repeatable presentation to measurement equipment

production_volume_range.basis: Inspection frequency and measurement plan govern fixture effort.

expected_datum_opportunities: features aligned to an approved inspection datum strategy
support_strategy: support without masking required inspection surfaces
locator_strategy: make datum simulation and verification authority explicit
stop_strategy: prevent wrong orientation without forcing non-datum surfaces
clamp_strategy: use only force needed for repeatable seating without measurement distortion
base_structure_strategy: stable base with explicit instrument or table interface
loading_unloading_model: load, seat, confirm, measure, release, and unload
access_expectations: probe, scanner, sight, calibration, and service access remain open
manufacturable_construction: stable, serviceable contacts and identifiable inspection features

configuration_parameters.name: inspection_plan_reference
configuration_parameters.description: Approved measurement-plan identity outside this template.

known_failure_modes: build datum is silently treated as inspection datum
unsupported_conditions: automatic pass or fail without metrology evidence
required_human_review_questions: Does the fixture represent the intended datum strategy?
Could fixture force bias the measurement?
Are calibration and uncertainty responsibilities explicit?

templates.name: Profile-check fixture
intended_product_family: formed or fabricated products reviewed against bounded profile regions

production_volume_range.basis: Sampling plan and tolerance intent drive construction.

expected_datum_opportunities: approved seating features independent of checked profile
support_strategy: support at non-checked regions or explicitly account for contact influence
locator_strategy: locate from the approved datum scheme, not the profile being checked
stop_strategy: foolproof orientation while leaving check regions visible
clamp_strategy: avoid forcing flexible product into a passing shape
base_structure_strategy: open plate or frame supporting replaceable profile references
loading_unloading_model: load without forcing, seat, compare, record, unload
access_expectations: all checked regions and gauges remain visible and reachable
manufacturable_construction: replaceable check segments with traceable nominal and allowance basis
process_context_needs: operator viewing and gauge access envelopes

configuration_parameters.name: checked_profile_regions
configuration_parameters.description: Explicit product regions and tolerance intent.

known_failure_modes: checked surface is also used as an uncontrolled locator
unsupported_conditions: missing profile tolerance and disposition rule
required_human_review_questions: Can the product be evaluated without being forced?
Are the profile reference and wear strategy maintainable?

templates.name: Go/no-go gauge
intended_product_family: products with an approved attribute suitable for bounded comparative gauging

production_volume_range.basis: Inspection frequency and gauge-control plan determine durability.

expected_datum_opportunities: approved gauge datum and controlled characteristic
support_strategy: support consistently without influencing the checked condition
locator_strategy: use the approved gauge datum definition
stop_strategy: separate go and no-go functions visibly
clamp_strategy: normally avoid force that can change the gauged condition
loading_unloading_model: identify gauge, clean, load, evaluate without force, record, unload
access_expectations: operator can observe disposition and maintain gauge surfaces
manufacturable_construction: wear-resistant replaceable gauge elements where approved
applicable_purchased_tooling_categories: indicators when used by the approved plan

configuration_parameters.name: gauge_disposition_definition
configuration_parameters.description: Licensed and approved inspection requirement not embedded by this template.

known_failure_modes: operator can force a reject through the go side
go and no-go identity is ambiguous
unsupported_conditions: no approved gauge design and calibration authority
required_human_review_questions: Who owns gauge design, calibration, and acceptance?
Can misuse or excessive force produce a false result?

templates.name: Rework fixture
intended_product_family: nonconforming or service products requiring controlled access to a defined repair

production_volume_range.basis: Expected recurrence and product variability govern reuse.

expected_datum_opportunities: surviving verified features not compromised by the defect
support_strategy: support current product condition without assuming nominal geometry
locator_strategy: use repair-relevant datums and allow known variation
stop_strategy: avoid relying on damaged or removed features
clamp_strategy: hold without worsening the defect or blocking repair
base_structure_strategy: adjustable or modular structure with locked and recorded settings
loading_unloading_model: identify condition, load, locate, repair, inspect, release, unload
access_expectations: repair tools, removed material, inspection, and safe handling are explicit
manufacturable_construction: bounded adjustment and replaceable condition-specific contacts
known_failure_modes: adjustment is not locked or recorded
unsupported_conditions: unknown product condition that affects safe support
required_human_review_questions: Does the fixture accommodate the actual nonconforming condition?
Can repair proceed without creating additional damage?

templates.name: Machining and workholding setup
intended_product_family: parts needing bounded workholding and machine-context fit review

production_volume_range.basis: Setup count, cutting plan, and repeatability govern complexity.

expected_datum_opportunities: machining datums and verified stock surfaces
support_strategy: support cutting reactions without hidden overconstraint
locator_strategy: align to the approved machining setup datum strategy
clamp_strategy: keep clamps clear of tool paths and direct force into supported regions
base_structure_strategy: pallet, vise, table-grid, or custom base interface recorded explicitly
loading_unloading_model: load, locate, clamp, verify, machine, release, unload
access_expectations: tool, spindle, holder, chips, coolant, probing, doors, and service are considered where relevant
manufacturable_construction: standard workholding before avoidable custom machining
process_context_needs: door and loading region when constrained

configuration_parameters.name: machine_context_identity
configuration_parameters.description: Exact or explicitly provisional machine context.

known_failure_modes: fixture fits nominal table but conflicts with tool or door
metadata-only vise is used for exact clearance
unsupported_conditions: cutting force or machine safety certification
required_human_review_questions: Is the workholding compatible with the actual machine and process?
Are cutting reactions and tool access acceptable?

templates.name: Product nest
intended_product_family: parts or assemblies needing repeatable support without necessarily full six-DOF restraint

production_volume_range.basis: Handling risk and reuse frequency govern durability.

support_strategy: minimum product-specific contacts needed for stable presentation
locator_strategy: only the motion required by the task is controlled
stop_strategy: orientation and loading cues are explicit
clamp_strategy: clamping is optional and separately justified
base_structure_strategy: compact plate, frame, or removable insert
loading_unloading_model: simple visible entry and lift-out or slide-out path
access_expectations: handling and downstream task access remain clear
manufacturable_construction: replaceable pads, cut plates, formed or machined inserts as justified
process_context_needs: only assets needed for the nest task

configuration_parameters.name: required_restraint
configuration_parameters.description: Motion that the nest must and must not control.

unsupported_conditions: unqualified full restraint or lifting claims
required_human_review_questions: Does the nest control only the motion needed?
Can the product be handled without damage or trapping?

templates.name: Combined build-and-check tooling
intended_product_family: assemblies where selected in-process checks are intentionally integrated with build tooling

production_volume_range.basis: Integration is justified only when it improves flow without destroying inspection independence.

expected_datum_opportunities: separately identified build and inspection datum relationships
support_strategy: build supports do not silently become measurement references
locator_strategy: each contact declares build, inspection, or shared role with justification
stop_strategy: loading stops remain distinct from acceptance features
clamp_strategy: inspection occurs in a declared clamp state and distortion risk is reviewed
base_structure_strategy: stable open structure supporting process and measurement access
loading_unloading_model: load, build, reach declared check state, inspect, release, unload
access_expectations: process and inspection equipment can reach without mutual interference
manufacturable_construction: replaceable and traceable inspection features separated from general fixture wear

configuration_parameters.name: inspection_state
configuration_parameters.description: Exact fixture and clamp state in which inspection evidence is valid.

known_failure_modes: fixture forces product into a passing condition
inspection and build roles are conflated
unsupported_conditions: automatic acceptance without approved measurement authority
required_human_review_questions: Is the check independent enough to be meaningful?
Does clamp or process state bias the result?
How are check features calibrated and maintained?
